finding: The Cloudflare evidence path had cryptographic JWT verification and an accepted workflow-context receipt, but no exact authorization step binding verified GitHub OIDC claims to that receipt. A deny-by-default claim authorizer now compares issuer, audience, environment subject, token time bounds, repository identity, workflow identity, ref, event, run identity, actor identity, and source SHA.
evidence_strength: deterministic local unit test of committed source interface; no live GitHub-issued token or Cloudflare deployment
falsification_route: Present a cryptographically verified GitHub OIDC claim set with any mismatched bound identity or invalid temporal/audience/subject condition and show that the authorizer accepts it, or present an exact valid set and show rejection.
privacy_boundary: Contains no token, secret, private chat, health information, or identifying personal data.
trust_limit: This layer authorizes claim semantics only after signature verification. It does not itself verify signatures, retrieve metadata, reject replay, prove mutation, prove deployment, or establish scientific or medical truth.
